name: Darnell Mooney
headline: Bounce-back upside buried behind Nabers in New York
body:
The two-year trend is jarring. In 2024 Mooney was a legitimate weapon: 992 yards and five scores on a 19.7% target share, with a 0.414 EPA per play, a 53.8% success rate and 1.92 yards per route run that ranked him 23rd. An injury-marred 2025 erased all of it, as his catch rate cratered to 44.4%, his EPA flatlined at 0.051, his success rate fell to 37.5%, and his yards per route halved to 1.00 (WR64). He still flashed the deep speed that produced a 14.2 air-yards-per-target mark, but the efficiency disappeared.

A one-year Giants deal reunites him with Matt Nagy and plugs him into Jaxson Dart's offense, but the path is narrow behind Malik Nabers, with Darius Slayton and Malachi Fields also in the mix. Consensus pegs him near 535 yards and three touchdowns, a WR3/WR4 line at best. His clearest route to relevance is a Nabers absence; otherwise he's a late-round dart throw.
